scope: artifact-only/read-only; no live recall, no DB/Docker mutation, no full/cumulative LOCOMO run
interpretation: For rows with missing gold atoms, preserved packet candidate pools generally do not contain literal missing atoms under current telemetry.
Several failures are not candidate-pool absence: row 163 has all gold atoms in context but answers the wrong subject; row 185 has gold atoms plus guitar conflict and is answer/benchmark ambiguity.

Some gold evidence exists in raw conv-26 dialogue but is absent from the preserved candidate pool, indicating upstream context_packet candidate generation/recall/query selection rather than the post-filter expansion gate.
Rows with inference-style gold answers and no explicit evidence cannot be recovered by literal candidate-pool matching alone.
